#!/bin/bash
#
# Copyright 2026 Scale Invariant, Inc.
#
# Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
# you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
# You may obtain a copy of the License at
#
#     http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
#
# Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
# distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
# WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
# See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
# limitations under the License.
#
# Author: Brad Hyslop <bhyslop@scaleinvariant.org>
#
# Recipe Bottle Lode - lifecycle REST (guard-free cluster, sourced by rbld0_Lode):
#   divine — enumerate Lodes / inspect one Lode's members (read-only)
#   banish — delete a whole Lode (Director credentials)

set -euo pipefail

######################################################################
# External Functions (rbld_*)

rbld_divine() {
  zrbld_sentinel

  local -r z_touchmark="${BUZ_FOLIO:-}"

  buc_doc_brief "Divine Lodes — enumerate all Lodes, or inspect one Lode's members (read-only)"
  buc_doc_oparm "touchmark" "Lode stamp to inspect (e.g., b260602120000); omit to enumerate all Lodes"
  buc_doc_shown || return 0

  buc_step "Authenticating as Director"
  test -f "${RBDC_DIRECTOR_RBRA_FILE}" \
    || buc_die "Director credential not found: ${RBDC_DIRECTOR_RBRA_FILE}"
  local z_token=""
  z_token=$(rbgo_get_token_capture "${RBDC_DIRECTOR_RBRA_FILE}") \
    || buc_die "Failed to get Director OAuth token"

  if test -z "${z_touchmark}"; then
    buc_step "Enumerating Lodes under ${RBGL_LODES_ROOT}/"
    zrbfc_list_anchors_capture "${z_token}" "${RBGL_LODES_ROOT}"

    if ! test -s "${ZRBFC_PACKAGE_LIST_FILE}"; then
      buc_info "No Lodes found under ${RBGL_LODES_ROOT}/"
      buc_success "Divine complete — 0 Lodes"
      return 0
    fi

    # Kind-letter legend, printed once so rows carry no repeated per-row column.
    # A touchmark's leading letter is its kind (b260602075327 -> bole); the
    # reader decodes the prefix from this key. One entry per implemented kind.
    local -r z_kind_fmt="    %-3s %-10s %s\n"
    echo ""
    printf "  Kinds (touchmark prefix):\n"
    printf "${z_kind_fmt}" "${RBGC_LODE_KIND_BOLE}" "bole" "upstream OCI image, consumed as a FROM line"

    # Load the touchmark list fully before iterating: the per-Lode tags fetch
    # spawns curl (via rbuh), and a child touching stdin would consume the
    # loop's remaining input. Load-then-iterate keeps that FD closed.
    local z_touchmarks=()
    local z_touch=""
    while IFS= read -r z_touch || test -n "${z_touch}"; do
      test -n "${z_touch}" || continue
      z_touchmarks+=("${z_touch}")
    done < "${ZRBFC_PACKAGE_LIST_FILE}"

    local -r z_row_fmt="  %-15s %s\n"
    echo ""
    printf "${z_row_fmt}" "TOUCHMARK" "IMAGE"
    printf "${z_row_fmt}" "---------------" "--------------------------------------"

    local z_idx=0
    local z_pkg=""
    local z_pkg_encoded=""
    local z_tags_url=""
    local z_enum_infix=""
    local z_resp_file=""
    local z_image_file=""
    local z_image=""
    for z_idx in "${!z_touchmarks[@]}"; do
      z_touch="${z_touchmarks[$z_idx]}"

      # One tags-list per Lode. The IMAGE column adapts to the Lode's member
      # scheme (the touchmark prefix names the kind; see the legend above). A
      # single-image Lode (bole) shows its unsprued fingerprint tag
      # <sanitized-origin>-<sha10>, located via the sha10 taken from the
      # rbi_sha256-<hex> member tag so Director semantic names (also unsprued)
      # cannot masquerade as the fingerprint. A clean-scheme cohort (reliquary)
      # carries no digest/fingerprint layer, so it has no single fingerprint to
      # show — report its member count instead (every non-:rbi_vouch tag is a
      # member). Per-Lode infix preserves each response for forensics.
      z_pkg="${RBGL_LODES_ROOT}/${z_touch}"
      z_pkg_encoded="${z_pkg//\//%2F}"
      z_tags_url="${ZRBFC_GAR_API_BASE}/${ZRBFC_GAR_PACKAGE_BASE}/packages/${z_pkg_encoded}/tags?pageSize=1000"
      z_enum_infix="rbld_divine_enum_${z_idx}"
      rbuh_json "GET" "${z_tags_url}" "${z_token}" "${z_enum_infix}"
      rbuh_require_ok "List tags for Lode ${z_touch}" "${z_enum_infix}"
      z_resp_file="${ZRBUH_PREFIX}${z_enum_infix}${ZRBUH_POSTFIX_JSON}"

      z_image_file="${ZRBLD_DIVINE_PREFIX}enum_${z_idx}_image.txt"
      jq -r --arg dp "${RBGC_LODE_TAG_DIGEST_PREFIX}" --arg vouch "${RBGC_LODE_TAG_VOUCH}" '
        [.tags[]?.name | sub(".*/tags/"; "")] as $names
        | ([$names[] | select(startswith($dp)) | ltrimstr($dp)[0:10]][0]) as $sha10
        | ([$names[] | select((startswith("rbi_") | not) and ($sha10 != null) and endswith("-" + $sha10))][0]) as $fingerprint
        | if $fingerprint != null then $fingerprint
          else "(cohort: \([$names[] | select(. != $vouch)] | length) members)"
          end
      ' "${z_resp_file}" > "${z_image_file}" \
        || buc_die "Failed to summarize Lode ${z_touch}"
      z_image=$(<"${z_image_file}")
      test -n "${z_image}" || buc_die "Empty summary extraction for Lode ${z_touch}"

      printf "${z_row_fmt}" "${z_touch}" "${z_image}"
    done
    echo ""
    buc_info "Total Lodes: ${#z_touchmarks[@]}"
    buc_success "Divine complete"
    return 0
  fi

  # Inspect depth: list the member tags on one Lode package.
  buc_step "Inspecting Lode ${RBGL_LODES_ROOT}/${z_touchmark}"
  local -r z_pkg="${RBGL_LODES_ROOT}/${z_touchmark}"
  local -r z_pkg_encoded="${z_pkg//\//%2F}"
  local -r z_tags_url="${ZRBFC_GAR_API_BASE}/${ZRBFC_GAR_PACKAGE_BASE}/packages/${z_pkg_encoded}/tags?pageSize=1000"
  local -r z_tags_infix="rbld_divine_tags"

  rbuh_json "GET" "${z_tags_url}" "${z_token}" "${z_tags_infix}"
  rbuh_require_ok "List tags for Lode ${z_touchmark}" "${z_tags_infix}"

  local -r z_resp_file="${ZRBUH_PREFIX}${z_tags_infix}${ZRBUH_POSTFIX_JSON}"
  local -r z_tags_file="${ZRBLD_DIVINE_PREFIX}tags.txt"
  jq -r '.tags[]?.name | sub(".*/tags/"; "")' "${z_resp_file}" > "${z_tags_file}" \
    || buc_die "Failed to extract member tags for Lode ${z_touchmark}"

  if ! test -s "${z_tags_file}"; then
    buc_die "No member tags found under ${z_pkg} — Lode not present in registry"
  fi

  echo ""
  printf "  %s\n" "MEMBER-TAG"
  printf "  %s\n" "------------------------------"
  local z_count=0
  local z_tag=""
  while IFS= read -r z_tag || test -n "${z_tag}"; do
    test -n "${z_tag}" || continue
    printf "  %s\n" "${z_tag}"
    z_count=$((z_count + 1))
  done < "${z_tags_file}"
  echo ""
  buc_info "Total members: ${z_count}"
  buc_success "Divine complete — Lode ${z_touchmark}"
}

rbld_banish() {
  zrbld_sentinel

  local -r z_touchmark="${BUZ_FOLIO:-}"

  buc_doc_brief "Banish a Lode — delete the whole rbi_ld/<touchmark> GAR package"
  buc_doc_param "touchmark" "Lode stamp to delete (e.g., b260602120000)"
  buc_doc_shown || return 0

  test -n "${z_touchmark}" || buc_die "Touchmark parameter required"

  buc_step "Authenticating as Director"
  local z_token=""
  z_token=$(rbgo_get_token_capture "${RBDC_DIRECTOR_RBRA_FILE}") \
    || buc_die "Failed to get Director OAuth token"

  local -r z_pkg="${RBGL_LODES_ROOT}/${z_touchmark}"
  local -r z_pkg_encoded="${z_pkg//\//%2F}"

  # Verify presence before delete so banish reports a clean not-found.
  buc_step "Verifying Lode present: ${z_pkg}"
  local -r z_tags_url="${ZRBFC_GAR_API_BASE}/${ZRBFC_GAR_PACKAGE_BASE}/packages/${z_pkg_encoded}/tags?pageSize=1"
  local -r z_probe_infix="rbld_banish_probe"
  rbuh_json "GET" "${z_tags_url}" "${z_token}" "${z_probe_infix}"
  rbuh_require_ok "Probe Lode ${z_touchmark}" "${z_probe_infix}"

  local z_tag_count=""
  z_tag_count=$(rbuh_json_field_capture "${z_probe_infix}" '(.tags // []) | length') \
    || buc_die "Failed to count tags for ${z_pkg}"
  test "${z_tag_count}" -gt 0 \
    || buc_die "No Lode found at ${z_pkg} — nothing to banish"

  buc_require "Will banish the whole Lode ${z_pkg} (single packages delete)" "yes"

  # Single packages delete removes the package and all its member versions/tags
  # atomically. DELETE returns a long-running operation; trust 200 as accepted.
  buc_step "Deleting Lode package: ${z_pkg}"
  local -r z_del_url="${ZRBFC_GAR_API_BASE}/${ZRBFC_GAR_PACKAGE_BASE}/packages/${z_pkg_encoded}"
  local -r z_del_infix="rbld_banish_del"
  rbuh_json "DELETE" "${z_del_url}" "${z_token}" "${z_del_infix}"
  rbuh_require_ok "Delete Lode package ${z_pkg}" "${z_del_infix}"

  echo ""
  buc_success "Lode banished: ${z_touchmark}"
}

# eof

content.text: Now the cloud steps — the heart of what I'm absorbing. Let me read all four plus the shared snippets:

1	#!/bin/bash
2	# RBGJL Step 01: Ensconce a base image into a Lode (capture) via skopeo
3	# Builder: skopeo (from reliquary)
4	# Substitutions: _RBGL_GAR_HOST, _RBGL_GAR_PATH, _RBGL_LODES_ROOT,
5	#                _RBGL_TAG_BOLE, _RBGL_TAG_DIGEST_PREFIX,
6	#                _RBGL_TRUST_GRADE, _RBGL_VOUCH_SCHEMA, _RBGL_ACQUIRED_BY,
7	#                _RBGL_IMAGE_1_ORIGIN, _RBGL_IMAGE_2_ORIGIN, _RBGL_IMAGE_3_ORIGIN,
8	#                _RBGL_LODE_1_STAMP,   _RBGL_LODE_2_STAMP,   _RBGL_LODE_3_STAMP
9	#
10	# For each non-empty (ORIGIN, STAMP) slot: inspect upstream, measure the canonical
11	# digest, copy --all into ONE GAR package rbi_ld/<stamp>, then apply the member
12	# tags by GAR->GAR retag (dedups blobs). Author the provenance envelope and stage
13	# it for step 02 (the :rbi_vouch artifact) and for the host capture-file via
14	# /builder/outputs/output. Mason SA ambient auth via Cloud Build metadata server.
15	#
16	# Package shape:  <host>/<path>/<LODES_ROOT>/<stamp>            (one package = one Lode)
17	# Member tags on that package, all pointing at the base manifest:
18	#   :<TAG_DIGEST_PREFIX><full-hex>   canonical OCI digest (exact cross-Lode dedup)
19	#   :<TAG_BOLE>                      uniform greppable handle
20	#   :<sanitized-origin>-<sha10>      UNSPRUED — name + glance-fingerprint (= legacy enshrine anchor)
21	# The :rbi_vouch tag is a separate manifest pushed by step 02.
22	
23	set -euo pipefail
24	echo "=== Ensconce base images into Lodes ==="
25	
26	# Obtain OAuth2 token from metadata server (Mason SA) — shared library snippet.
27	#@rbgjs_include token-fetch
28	
29	# CB substitutions are expanded at submit time, not available as shell variables.
30	# Capture each into a runtime variable so we can loop.
31	SLOT_1_ORIGIN="${_RBGL_IMAGE_1_ORIGIN}"
32	SLOT_2_ORIGIN="${_RBGL_IMAGE_2_ORIGIN}"
33	SLOT_3_ORIGIN="${_RBGL_IMAGE_3_ORIGIN}"
34	SLOT_1_STAMP="${_RBGL_LODE_1_STAMP}"
35	SLOT_2_STAMP="${_RBGL_LODE_2_STAMP}"
36	SLOT_3_STAMP="${_RBGL_LODE_3_STAMP}"
37	
38	# Acquisition moment, attested once for the whole build.
39	ACQUIRED_AT=$(date -u +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ)
40	
41	# Stamp roster for step 02 (one line per captured Lode).
42	: > /workspace/lode_stamps.txt
43	
44	# Initialize result JSON (host reads this back as the capture-file source)
45	RESULT='{'
46	FIRST=true
47	
48	for SLOT in 1 2 3; do
49	  case "${SLOT}" in
50	    1) ORIGIN="${SLOT_1_ORIGIN}"; STAMP="${SLOT_1_STAMP}" ;;
51	    2) ORIGIN="${SLOT_2_ORIGIN}"; STAMP="${SLOT_2_STAMP}" ;;
52	    3) ORIGIN="${SLOT_3_ORIGIN}"; STAMP="${SLOT_3_STAMP}" ;;
53	  esac
54	  test -n "${ORIGIN}" || continue
55	  test -n "${STAMP}"  || { echo "FATAL: slot ${SLOT} has ORIGIN but no STAMP" >&2; exit 1; }
56	
57	  echo "--- Slot ${SLOT}: ${ORIGIN} -> ${_RBGL_LODES_ROOT}/${STAMP} ---"
58	
59	  # Inspect upstream, take the canonical digest, derive the glance fingerprint —
60	  # shared library snippet (requires ORIGIN + RAW_FILE; provides SHA + FINGERPRINT).
61	  RAW_FILE="/workspace/ensconce_raw_${SLOT}.json"
62	#@rbgjs_include skopeo-fingerprint
63	
64	  DIGEST_TAG="${_RBGL_TAG_DIGEST_PREFIX}${SHA}"
65	  PKG="${_RBGL_GAR_HOST}/${_RBGL_GAR_PATH}/${_RBGL_LODES_ROOT}/${STAMP}"
66	
67	  echo "Package: ${PKG}"
68	  echo "Digest:  sha256:${SHA}"
69	  echo "Tags:    ${DIGEST_TAG}, ${_RBGL_TAG_BOLE}, ${FINGERPRINT}"
70	
71	  # Collision guard — never silently clobber an existing Lode. The bole handle
72	  # (:rbi_bole) always points at this touchmark's captured image, so inspect it:
73	  # absent => a fresh touchmark; present => re-use. Re-use is legitimate ONLY when
74	  # it is the identical canonical digest (a Cloud Build retry re-copying the same
75	  # bytes); a different digest under the same touchmark is a clobber and fails loud.
76	  # The check sits immediately before the copy in this same step — atomic with the
77	  # GAR write in the sense the spec requires (no host pre-submit window). EXISTING_SHA
78	  # is computed the same way as SHA (sha256sum of the raw manifest), so the compare is
79	  # apples-to-apples. An inspect failure is treated as absent: cloud-side auth/network
80	  # is reliable, and a genuine GAR outage fails the copy below regardless.
81	  EXISTING_RAW="/workspace/ensconce_existing_${SLOT}.json"
82	  if skopeo inspect --raw "docker://${PKG}:${_RBGL_TAG_BOLE}" \
83	       --creds "oauth2accesstoken:${TOKEN}" > "${EXISTING_RAW}" 2>/dev/null; then
84	    EXISTING_SHA=$(sha256sum "${EXISTING_RAW}" | cut -d' ' -f1)
85	    if [ "${EXISTING_SHA}" = "${SHA}" ]; then
86	      echo "Touchmark ${STAMP} already holds identical digest sha256:${SHA} — idempotent retry, proceeding."
87	    else
88	      echo "FATAL: touchmark collision at ${PKG}" >&2
89	      echo "  existing :${_RBGL_TAG_BOLE} -> sha256:${EXISTING_SHA}" >&2
90	      echo "  refusing to clobber with  -> sha256:${SHA}" >&2
91	      echo "  (banish the Lode first, or ensconce under a fresh touchmark)" >&2
92	      exit 1
93	    fi
94	  else
95	    echo "Touchmark ${STAMP} is fresh (no existing :${_RBGL_TAG_BOLE})."
96	  fi
97	
98	  # Copy upstream into the Lode package under the canonical digest tag.
99	  skopeo copy --all \
100	    "docker://${ORIGIN}" \
101	    "docker://${PKG}:${DIGEST_TAG}" \
102	    --dest-creds "oauth2accesstoken:${TOKEN}" \
103	    || { echo "FATAL: skopeo copy failed for slot ${SLOT}" >&2; exit 1; }
104	
105	  # Apply remaining member tags by GAR->GAR retag (same blobs, manifest re-tag only).
106	  for MEMBER_TAG in "${_RBGL_TAG_BOLE}" "${FINGERPRINT}"; do
107	    skopeo copy --all \
108	      "docker://${PKG}:${DIGEST_TAG}" \
109	      "docker://${PKG}:${MEMBER_TAG}" \
110	      --src-creds  "oauth2accesstoken:${TOKEN}" \
111	      --dest-creds "oauth2accesstoken:${TOKEN}" \
112	      || { echo "FATAL: skopeo retag ${MEMBER_TAG} failed for slot ${SLOT}" >&2; exit 1; }
113	  done
114	
115	  echo "Slot ${SLOT} ensconced: ${STAMP}"
116	
117	  # Author the provenance envelope (identical content lands in :rbi_vouch and the
118	  # host capture-file). No jq dependency — values are controlled (sanitized origin,
119	  # hex digest, SA email, build id, ISO timestamp); none can carry a literal quote.
120	  # members[] is the cardinality axis — length 1 for the bole singleton.
121	  ENVELOPE='{'
122	  ENVELOPE="${ENVELOPE}\"schema\":\"${_RBGL_VOUCH_SCHEMA}\","
123	  ENVELOPE="${ENVELOPE}\"kind\":\"bole\","
124	  ENVELOPE="${ENVELOPE}\"lode\":\"${STAMP}\","
125	  ENVELOPE="${ENVELOPE}\"acquired_at\":\"${ACQUIRED_AT}\","
126	  ENVELOPE="${ENVELOPE}\"acquired_by\":\"${_RBGL_ACQUIRED_BY}\","
127	  ENVELOPE="${ENVELOPE}\"capture_build\":\"${BUILD_ID:-}\","
128	  ENVELOPE="${ENVELOPE}\"trust_grade\":\"${_RBGL_TRUST_GRADE}\","
129	  ENVELOPE="${ENVELOPE}\"signature\":null,"
130	  ENVELOPE="${ENVELOPE}\"members\":[{"
131	  ENVELOPE="${ENVELOPE}\"name\":\"${_RBGL_TAG_BOLE}\","
132	  ENVELOPE="${ENVELOPE}\"origin\":\"${ORIGIN}\","
133	  ENVELOPE="${ENVELOPE}\"digest\":\"sha256:${SHA}\","
134	  ENVELOPE="${ENVELOPE}\"verification\":\"oci-digest\","
135	  ENVELOPE="${ENVELOPE}\"tags\":[\"${_RBGL_TAG_BOLE}\",\"${DIGEST_TAG}\",\"${FINGERPRINT}\"]"
136	  ENVELOPE="${ENVELOPE}}]}"
137	
138	  # Stage the envelope for step 02 (pushes it as the :rbi_vouch artifact).
139	  printf '%s' "${ENVELOPE}" > "/workspace/lode_${STAMP}_vouch.json"
140	  echo "${STAMP}" >> /workspace/lode_stamps.txt
141	
142	  # Accumulate host-facing result (the capture-file carries the same envelope).
143	  if [ "${FIRST}" = "true" ]; then
144	    FIRST=false
145	  else
146	    RESULT="${RESULT},"
147	  fi
148	  RESULT="${RESULT}\"slot_${SLOT}\":{\"stamp\":\"${STAMP}\",\"vouch\":${ENVELOPE}}"
149	done
150	
151	RESULT="${RESULT}}"
152	
153	echo "=== Writing capture results ==="
154	echo "${RESULT}"
155	
156	# Write to buildStepOutputs channel (host extracts per-Lode envelope -> capture-file)
157	mkdir -p /builder/outputs
158	printf '%s' "${RESULT}" > /builder/outputs/output
159	
160	echo "=== Ensconce capture step complete ==="
161

#!/bin/bash
# RBGJL Step 01: Ensconce a base image into a Lode (capture) via skopeo
# Builder: skopeo (from reliquary)
# Substitutions: _RBGL_GAR_HOST, _RBGL_GAR_PATH, _RBGL_LODES_ROOT,
#                _RBGL_TAG_BOLE, _RBGL_TAG_DIGEST_PREFIX,
#                _RBGL_TRUST_GRADE, _RBGL_VOUCH_SCHEMA, _RBGL_ACQUIRED_BY,
#                _RBGL_IMAGE_1_ORIGIN, _RBGL_IMAGE_2_ORIGIN, _RBGL_IMAGE_3_ORIGIN,
#                _RBGL_LODE_1_STAMP,   _RBGL_LODE_2_STAMP,   _RBGL_LODE_3_STAMP
#
# For each non-empty (ORIGIN, STAMP) slot: inspect upstream, measure the canonical
# digest, copy --all into ONE GAR package rbi_ld/<stamp>, then apply the member
# tags by GAR->GAR retag (dedups blobs). Author the provenance envelope and stage
# it for step 02 (the :rbi_vouch artifact) and for the host capture-file via
# /builder/outputs/output. Mason SA ambient auth via Cloud Build metadata server.
#
# Package shape:  <host>/<path>/<LODES_ROOT>/<stamp>            (one package = one Lode)
# Member tags on that package, all pointing at the base manifest:
#   :<TAG_DIGEST_PREFIX><full-hex>   canonical OCI digest (exact cross-Lode dedup)
#   :<TAG_BOLE>                      uniform greppable handle
#   :<sanitized-origin>-<sha10>      UNSPRUED — name + glance-fingerprint (= legacy enshrine anchor)
# The :rbi_vouch tag is a separate manifest pushed by step 02.

set -euo pipefail
echo "=== Ensconce base images into Lodes ==="

# Obtain OAuth2 token from metadata server (Mason SA) — shared library snippet.
#@rbgjs_include token-fetch

# CB substitutions are expanded at submit time, not available as shell variables.
# Capture each into a runtime variable so we can loop.
SLOT_1_ORIGIN="${_RBGL_IMAGE_1_ORIGIN}"
SLOT_2_ORIGIN="${_RBGL_IMAGE_2_ORIGIN}"
SLOT_3_ORIGIN="${_RBGL_IMAGE_3_ORIGIN}"
SLOT_1_STAMP="${_RBGL_LODE_1_STAMP}"
SLOT_2_STAMP="${_RBGL_LODE_2_STAMP}"
SLOT_3_STAMP="${_RBGL_LODE_3_STAMP}"

# Acquisition moment, attested once for the whole build.
ACQUIRED_AT=$(date -u +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ)

# Stamp roster for step 02 (one line per captured Lode).
: > /workspace/lode_stamps.txt

# Initialize result JSON (host reads this back as the capture-file source)
RESULT='{'
FIRST=true

for SLOT in 1 2 3; do
  case "${SLOT}" in
    1) ORIGIN="${SLOT_1_ORIGIN}"; STAMP="${SLOT_1_STAMP}" ;;
    2) ORIGIN="${SLOT_2_ORIGIN}"; STAMP="${SLOT_2_STAMP}" ;;
    3) ORIGIN="${SLOT_3_ORIGIN}"; STAMP="${SLOT_3_STAMP}" ;;
  esac
  test -n "${ORIGIN}" || continue
  test -n "${STAMP}"  || { echo "FATAL: slot ${SLOT} has ORIGIN but no STAMP" >&2; exit 1; }

  echo "--- Slot ${SLOT}: ${ORIGIN} -> ${_RBGL_LODES_ROOT}/${STAMP} ---"

  # Inspect upstream, take the canonical digest, derive the glance fingerprint —
  # shared library snippet (requires ORIGIN + RAW_FILE; provides SHA + FINGERPRINT).
  RAW_FILE="/workspace/ensconce_raw_${SLOT}.json"
#@rbgjs_include skopeo-fingerprint

  DIGEST_TAG="${_RBGL_TAG_DIGEST_PREFIX}${SHA}"
  PKG="${_RBGL_GAR_HOST}/${_RBGL_GAR_PATH}/${_RBGL_LODES_ROOT}/${STAMP}"

  echo "Package: ${PKG}"
  echo "Digest:  sha256:${SHA}"
  echo "Tags:    ${DIGEST_TAG}, ${_RBGL_TAG_BOLE}, ${FINGERPRINT}"

  # Collision guard — never silently clobber an existing Lode. The bole handle
  # (:rbi_bole) always points at this touchmark's captured image, so inspect it:
  # absent => a fresh touchmark; present => re-use. Re-use is legitimate ONLY when
  # it is the identical canonical digest (a Cloud Build retry re-copying the same
  # bytes); a different digest under the same touchmark is a clobber and fails loud.
  # The check sits immediately before the copy in this same step — atomic with the
  # GAR write in the sense the spec requires (no host pre-submit window). EXISTING_SHA
  # is computed the same way as SHA (sha256sum of the raw manifest), so the compare is
  # apples-to-apples. An inspect failure is treated as absent: cloud-side auth/network
  # is reliable, and a genuine GAR outage fails the copy below regardless.
  EXISTING_RAW="/workspace/ensconce_existing_${SLOT}.json"
  if skopeo inspect --raw "docker://${PKG}:${_RBGL_TAG_BOLE}" \
       --creds "oauth2accesstoken:${TOKEN}" > "${EXISTING_RAW}" 2>/dev/null; then
    EXISTING_SHA=$(sha256sum "${EXISTING_RAW}" | cut -d' ' -f1)
    if [ "${EXISTING_SHA}" = "${SHA}" ]; then
      echo "Touchmark ${STAMP} already holds identical digest sha256:${SHA} — idempotent retry, proceeding."
    else
      echo "FATAL: touchmark collision at ${PKG}" >&2
      echo "  existing :${_RBGL_TAG_BOLE} -> sha256:${EXISTING_SHA}" >&2
      echo "  refusing to clobber with  -> sha256:${SHA}" >&2
      echo "  (banish the Lode first, or ensconce under a fresh touchmark)" >&2
      exit 1
    fi
  else
    echo "Touchmark ${STAMP} is fresh (no existing :${_RBGL_TAG_BOLE})."
  fi

  # Copy upstream into the Lode package under the canonical digest tag.
  skopeo copy --all \
    "docker://${ORIGIN}" \
    "docker://${PKG}:${DIGEST_TAG}" \
    --dest-creds "oauth2accesstoken:${TOKEN}" \
    || { echo "FATAL: skopeo copy failed for slot ${SLOT}" >&2; exit 1; }

  # Apply remaining member tags by GAR->GAR retag (same blobs, manifest re-tag only).
  for MEMBER_TAG in "${_RBGL_TAG_BOLE}" "${FINGERPRINT}"; do
    skopeo copy --all \
      "docker://${PKG}:${DIGEST_TAG}" \
      "docker://${PKG}:${MEMBER_TAG}" \
      --src-creds  "oauth2accesstoken:${TOKEN}" \
      --dest-creds "oauth2accesstoken:${TOKEN}" \
      || { echo "FATAL: skopeo retag ${MEMBER_TAG} failed for slot ${SLOT}" >&2; exit 1; }
  done

  echo "Slot ${SLOT} ensconced: ${STAMP}"

  # Author the provenance envelope (identical content lands in :rbi_vouch and the
  # host capture-file). No jq dependency — values are controlled (sanitized origin,
  # hex digest, SA email, build id, ISO timestamp); none can carry a literal quote.
  # members[] is the cardinality axis — length 1 for the bole singleton.
  ENVELOPE='{'
  ENVELOPE="${ENVELOPE}\"schema\":\"${_RBGL_VOUCH_SCHEMA}\","
  ENVELOPE="${ENVELOPE}\"kind\":\"bole\","
  ENVELOPE="${ENVELOPE}\"lode\":\"${STAMP}\","
  ENVELOPE="${ENVELOPE}\"acquired_at\":\"${ACQUIRED_AT}\","
  ENVELOPE="${ENVELOPE}\"acquired_by\":\"${_RBGL_ACQUIRED_BY}\","
  ENVELOPE="${ENVELOPE}\"capture_build\":\"${BUILD_ID:-}\","
  ENVELOPE="${ENVELOPE}\"trust_grade\":\"${_RBGL_TRUST_GRADE}\","
  ENVELOPE="${ENVELOPE}\"signature\":null,"
  ENVELOPE="${ENVELOPE}\"members\":[{"
  ENVELOPE="${ENVELOPE}\"name\":\"${_RBGL_TAG_BOLE}\","
  ENVELOPE="${ENVELOPE}\"origin\":\"${ORIGIN}\","
  ENVELOPE="${ENVELOPE}\"digest\":\"sha256:${SHA}\","
  ENVELOPE="${ENVELOPE}\"verification\":\"oci-digest\","
  ENVELOPE="${ENVELOPE}\"tags\":[\"${_RBGL_TAG_BOLE}\",\"${DIGEST_TAG}\",\"${FINGERPRINT}\"]"
  ENVELOPE="${ENVELOPE}}]}"

  # Stage the envelope for step 02 (pushes it as the :rbi_vouch artifact).
  printf '%s' "${ENVELOPE}" > "/workspace/lode_${STAMP}_vouch.json"
  echo "${STAMP}" >> /workspace/lode_stamps.txt

  # Accumulate host-facing result (the capture-file carries the same envelope).
  if [ "${FIRST}" = "true" ]; then
    FIRST=false
  else
    RESULT="${RESULT},"
  fi
  RESULT="${RESULT}\"slot_${SLOT}\":{\"stamp\":\"${STAMP}\",\"vouch\":${ENVELOPE}}"
done

RESULT="${RESULT}}"

echo "=== Writing capture results ==="
echo "${RESULT}"

# Write to buildStepOutputs channel (host extracts per-Lode envelope -> capture-file)
mkdir -p /builder/outputs
printf '%s' "${RESULT}" > /builder/outputs/output

echo "=== Ensconce capture step complete ==="

2	# RBGJL Step 02: Push each Lode's provenance envelope as its :rbi_vouch artifact
3	# Builder: docker (from reliquary)
4	# Entrypoint: bash
5	# Substitutions: _RBGL_GAR_HOST, _RBGL_GAR_PATH, _RBGL_LODES_ROOT, _RBGL_TAG_VOUCH
6	#
7	# Note: The Dockerfile echo-sequence below is intentional — this script runs
8	# inside a Cloud Build container, not under BCG module discipline.
9	#

11	# /workspace/lode_<stamp>_vouch.json and listed the stamps in
12	# /workspace/lode_stamps.txt. Each envelope rides into the SAME package as the
13	# base manifest, under the :rbi_vouch tag — a distinct manifest, one per Lode.
14	# Vouch content is architecture-independent; single-platform is sufficient.

34	  test -f "${ENVELOPE_FILE}" \

44	  echo "COPY vouch.json /"   >> "${CTX}/Dockerfile"

46	  # Push the FROM-scratch vouch context — shared library snippet.
47	  PUSH_URI="${VOUCH_URI}"

#!/bin/bash
# RBGJL Step 02: Push each Lode's provenance envelope as its :rbi_vouch artifact
# Builder: docker (from reliquary)
# Entrypoint: bash
# Substitutions: _RBGL_GAR_HOST, _RBGL_GAR_PATH, _RBGL_LODES_ROOT, _RBGL_TAG_VOUCH
#
# Note: The Dockerfile echo-sequence below is intentional — this script runs
# inside a Cloud Build container, not under BCG module discipline.
#
# Step 01 (skopeo) staged one envelope per captured Lode at
# /workspace/lode_<stamp>_vouch.json and listed the stamps in
# /workspace/lode_stamps.txt. Each envelope rides into the SAME package as the
# base manifest, under the :rbi_vouch tag — a distinct manifest, one per Lode.
# Vouch content is architecture-independent; single-platform is sufficient.

set -euo pipefail

echo "=== Assemble and push Lode vouch artifacts ==="

test -f /workspace/lode_stamps.txt \
  || { echo "FATAL: /workspace/lode_stamps.txt not found — step 01 must run first" >&2; exit 1; }
test -s /workspace/lode_stamps.txt \
  || { echo "FATAL: /workspace/lode_stamps.txt is empty — nothing ensconced" >&2; exit 1; }

test -n "${_RBGL_TAG_VOUCH}" || { echo "FATAL: _RBGL_TAG_VOUCH missing" >&2; exit 1; }

# Ensure the shared buildx builder — shared library snippet (run once).
#@rbgjs_include buildx-bootstrap

while IFS= read -r STAMP || test -n "${STAMP}"; do
  test -n "${STAMP}" || continue

  ENVELOPE_FILE="/workspace/lode_${STAMP}_vouch.json"
  test -f "${ENVELOPE_FILE}" \
    || { echo "FATAL: envelope not staged for ${STAMP}: ${ENVELOPE_FILE}" >&2; exit 1; }

  VOUCH_URI="${_RBGL_GAR_HOST}/${_RBGL_GAR_PATH}/${_RBGL_LODES_ROOT}/${STAMP}:${_RBGL_TAG_VOUCH}"
  echo "--- Vouch for ${STAMP} -> ${VOUCH_URI} ---"

  CTX="/workspace/vouch_ctx_${STAMP}"
  mkdir -p "${CTX}"
  cp "${ENVELOPE_FILE}" "${CTX}/vouch.json"
  echo "FROM scratch"        >  "${CTX}/Dockerfile"
  echo "COPY vouch.json /"   >> "${CTX}/Dockerfile"

  # Push the FROM-scratch vouch context — shared library snippet.
  PUSH_URI="${VOUCH_URI}"
  PUSH_PLATFORMS="linux/amd64"
  PUSH_CTX="${CTX}"
#@rbgjs_include buildx-push

  echo "Vouch pushed: ${VOUCH_URI}"
done < /workspace/lode_stamps.txt

echo "=== Vouch push step complete ==="

2	# RBGJI Step 01: Inscribe tool images to GAR reliquary
3	# Builder: gcr.io/cloud-builders/docker (always pullable — Google-hosted)
4	# Substitutions: _RBGN_GAR_HOST, _RBGN_GAR_PATH,
5	#                _RBGN_RELIQUARIES_ROOT, _RBGN_RELIQUARY
6	#
7	# Mirrors all 6 GCB step/tool images from upstream registries to a datestamped
8	# GAR namespace (reliquary). Co-versioned: all images in one pass, one datestamp.
9	# Single-platform (linux/amd64) — tool images run as GCB steps on amd64 workers.
10	# Outputs JSON manifest with tool→digest mapping via /builder/outputs/output.
11	#
12	# Image URI shape: <host>/<path>/<RELIQUARIES_ROOT>/<RELIQUARY>/<NAME>:<RELIQUARY>
13	# Tag = the reliquary datestamp itself (immutable-datestamp-as-tag).
14	
15	set -euo pipefail
16	echo "=== Inscribe tool images to GAR reliquary ==="
17	echo "Reliquary: ${_RBGN_RELIQUARY}"
18	
19	# Tool image manifest: short-name|upstream-ref
20	# This is the authoritative list of tool images for GCB step execution.
21	# gcloud and docker are Google-hosted (gcr.io); rest are third-party.
22	MANIFEST=(
23	  "gcloud|gcr.io/cloud-builders/gcloud:latest"
24	  "docker|gcr.io/cloud-builders/docker:latest"
25	  "alpine|docker.io/library/alpine:latest"
26	  "syft|docker.io/anchore/syft:latest"
27	  "binfmt|docker.io/tonistiigi/binfmt:latest"
28	  "skopeo|quay.io/skopeo/stable:latest"
29	)
30	
31	RESULT='{'
32	FIRST=true
33	
34	for ENTRY in "${MANIFEST[@]}"; do
35	  NAME="${ENTRY%%|*}"
36	  UPSTREAM="${ENTRY#*|}"
37	
38	  echo "--- ${NAME}: ${UPSTREAM} ---"
39	
40	  DEST="${_RBGN_GAR_HOST}/${_RBGN_GAR_PATH}/${_RBGN_RELIQUARIES_ROOT}/${_RBGN_RELIQUARY}/${NAME}:${_RBGN_RELIQUARY}"
41	  echo "Dest: ${DEST}"
42	
43	  docker pull "${UPSTREAM}" \
44	    || { echo "FATAL: Failed to pull ${UPSTREAM}" >&2; exit 1; }
45	
46	  # Extract pulled digest from RepoDigests (format: repo@sha256:...)
47	  DIGEST=$(docker inspect --format='{{index .RepoDigests 0}}' "${UPSTREAM}" 2>/dev/null) || DIGEST=""
48	  DIGEST="${DIGEST##*@}"
49	
50	  docker tag "${UPSTREAM}" "${DEST}" \
51	    || { echo "FATAL: Failed to tag ${NAME}" >&2; exit 1; }
52	
53	  docker push "${DEST}" \
54	    || { echo "FATAL: Failed to push ${NAME} to GAR" >&2; exit 1; }
55	
56	  echo "${NAME} inscribed: ${DEST} (${DIGEST})"
57	
58	  # Accumulate JSON result
59	  if [ "${FIRST}" = "true" ]; then
60	    FIRST=false
61	  else
62	    RESULT="${RESULT},"
63	  fi
64	  RESULT="${RESULT}\"${NAME}\":{\"upstream\":\"${UPSTREAM}\",\"digest\":\"${DIGEST}\"}"
65	done
66	
67	RESULT="${RESULT}}"
68	
69	echo "=== Writing inscribe results ==="
70	echo "${RESULT}"
71	
72	mkdir -p /builder/outputs
73	printf '%s' "${RESULT}" > /builder/outputs/output
74	
75	echo "=== Inscribe step complete ==="

#!/bin/bash
# RBGJI Step 01: Inscribe tool images to GAR reliquary
# Builder: gcr.io/cloud-builders/docker (always pullable — Google-hosted)
# Substitutions: _RBGN_GAR_HOST, _RBGN_GAR_PATH,
#                _RBGN_RELIQUARIES_ROOT, _RBGN_RELIQUARY
#
# Mirrors all 6 GCB step/tool images from upstream registries to a datestamped
# GAR namespace (reliquary). Co-versioned: all images in one pass, one datestamp.
# Single-platform (linux/amd64) — tool images run as GCB steps on amd64 workers.
# Outputs JSON manifest with tool→digest mapping via /builder/outputs/output.
#
# Image URI shape: <host>/<path>/<RELIQUARIES_ROOT>/<RELIQUARY>/<NAME>:<RELIQUARY>
# Tag = the reliquary datestamp itself (immutable-datestamp-as-tag).

set -euo pipefail
echo "=== Inscribe tool images to GAR reliquary ==="
echo "Reliquary: ${_RBGN_RELIQUARY}"

# Tool image manifest: short-name|upstream-ref
# This is the authoritative list of tool images for GCB step execution.
# gcloud and docker are Google-hosted (gcr.io); rest are third-party.
MANIFEST=(
  "gcloud|gcr.io/cloud-builders/gcloud:latest"
  "docker|gcr.io/cloud-builders/docker:latest"
  "alpine|docker.io/library/alpine:latest"
  "syft|docker.io/anchore/syft:latest"
  "binfmt|docker.io/tonistiigi/binfmt:latest"
  "skopeo|quay.io/skopeo/stable:latest"
)

RESULT='{'
FIRST=true

for ENTRY in "${MANIFEST[@]}"; do
  NAME="${ENTRY%%|*}"
  UPSTREAM="${ENTRY#*|}"

  echo "--- ${NAME}: ${UPSTREAM} ---"

  DEST="${_RBGN_GAR_HOST}/${_RBGN_GAR_PATH}/${_RBGN_RELIQUARIES_ROOT}/${_RBGN_RELIQUARY}/${NAME}:${_RBGN_RELIQUARY}"
  echo "Dest: ${DEST}"

  docker pull "${UPSTREAM}" \
    || { echo "FATAL: Failed to pull ${UPSTREAM}" >&2; exit 1; }

  # Extract pulled digest from RepoDigests (format: repo@sha256:...)
  DIGEST=$(docker inspect --format='{{index .RepoDigests 0}}' "${UPSTREAM}" 2>/dev/null) || DIGEST=""
  DIGEST="${DIGEST##*@}"

  docker tag "${UPSTREAM}" "${DEST}" \
    || { echo "FATAL: Failed to tag ${NAME}" >&2; exit 1; }

  docker push "${DEST}" \
    || { echo "FATAL: Failed to push ${NAME} to GAR" >&2; exit 1; }

  echo "${NAME} inscribed: ${DEST} (${DIGEST})"

  # Accumulate JSON result
  if [ "${FIRST}" = "true" ]; then
    FIRST=false
  else
    RESULT="${RESULT},"
  fi
  RESULT="${RESULT}\"${NAME}\":{\"upstream\":\"${UPSTREAM}\",\"digest\":\"${DIGEST}\"}"
done

RESULT="${RESULT}}"

echo "=== Writing inscribe results ==="
echo "${RESULT}"

mkdir -p /builder/outputs
printf '%s' "${RESULT}" > /builder/outputs/output

echo "=== Inscribe step complete ==="

1	#!/bin/sh
2	# RBGJR Step 01: In-pool reliquary preflight — HEAD-check tool images and base anchors
3	# Builder: alpine (from reliquary)
4	# Substitutions: _RBGR_GAR_HOST, _RBGR_GAR_PATH, _RBGR_RELIQUARIES_ROOT,
5	#                _RBGR_RELIQUARY,
6	#                _RBGR_BASE_LOCATOR_1, _RBGR_BASE_LOCATOR_2, _RBGR_BASE_LOCATOR_3
7	#
8	# Defense-in-depth: every Cloud Build job validates reliquary GAR-presence
9	# from the worker pool's vantage before expensive work runs. Catches divergence
10	# the host-side preflight cannot — airgap pool private-VPC routing, IAM split
11	# between Director and Mason service accounts, time skew between submission and
12	# execution.
13	#
14	# Coverage:
15	#   - Reliquary tool images (5 of 6): gcloud, docker, syft, binfmt, skopeo.
16	#     Alpine (this step's own image) is implicitly validated by Cloud Build
17	#     pulling it for step execution; failure to pull alpine produces Cloud
18	#     Build's own "image not pullable" error before this script runs.
19	#   - Base anchor locators (slots 1/2/3 if non-empty). Conjure populates
20	#     these; bind and graft pass them through empty.
21	#
22	# Failure mode: structured "this pool can't see «TOOL»" message naming each
23	# missed item. Operator remediation: re-inscribe the reliquary, re-yoke the
24	# vessel, re-ordain the hallmark.
25	#
26	# Auth: Mason SA via Cloud Build metadata server. HEAD requests via busybox
27	# wget (no curl in alpine by default; apk add not viable on airgap pools).
28	# wget exit code is the binary signal — 0 for HTTP 2xx, non-zero otherwise.
29	
30	# POSIX sh (busybox ash on alpine): pipefail is non-portable and not load-bearing
31	# here — the one pipe (token extract) is validated by the test -n guard below.
32	set -eu
33	
34	test -n "${_RBGR_GAR_HOST}"          || { echo "FATAL: _RBGR_GAR_HOST missing"          >&2; exit 1; }
35	test -n "${_RBGR_GAR_PATH}"          || { echo "FATAL: _RBGR_GAR_PATH missing"          >&2; exit 1; }
36	test -n "${_RBGR_RELIQUARIES_ROOT}"  || { echo "FATAL: _RBGR_RELIQUARIES_ROOT missing"  >&2; exit 1; }
37	test -n "${_RBGR_RELIQUARY}"         || { echo "FATAL: _RBGR_RELIQUARY missing"         >&2; exit 1; }
38	
39	echo "=== In-pool reliquary preflight ==="
40	echo "Reliquary: ${_RBGR_RELIQUARY}"
41	echo "GAR base:  ${_RBGR_GAR_HOST}/${_RBGR_GAR_PATH}"
42	
43	echo "Fetching OAuth2 token from metadata server"
44	TOKEN_JSON=$(wget -q -O - \
45	  --header="Metadata-Flavor: Google" \
46	  "http://metadata.google.internal/computeMetadata/v1/instance/service-accounts/default/token") \
47	  || { echo "FATAL: Failed to fetch OAuth2 token from metadata server" >&2; exit 1; }
48	
49	TOKEN=$(printf '%s' "${TOKEN_JSON}" | grep -o '"access_token":"[^"]*"' | cut -d'"' -f4)
50	test -n "${TOKEN}" || { echo "FATAL: Failed to extract access_token from metadata response" >&2; exit 1; }
51	
52	ACCEPT_MTYPES="application/vnd.docker.distribution.manifest.v2+json,application/vnd.docker.distribution.manifest.list.v2+json,application/vnd.oci.image.index.v1+json,application/vnd.oci.image.manifest.v1+json"
53	
54	REGISTRY_API_BASE="https://${_RBGR_GAR_HOST}/v2/${_RBGR_GAR_PATH}"
55	
56	MISSES=""
57	
58	# Tool list mirrors the inscribe step manifest (rbgji01) minus alpine.
59	# Each tool's package path: <RELIQUARIES_ROOT>/<RELIQUARY>/<NAME>; tag = <RELIQUARY>.
60	for TOOL in gcloud docker syft binfmt skopeo; do
61	  PKG_PATH="${_RBGR_RELIQUARIES_ROOT}/${_RBGR_RELIQUARY}/${TOOL}"
62	  URL="${REGISTRY_API_BASE}/${PKG_PATH}/manifests/${_RBGR_RELIQUARY}"
63	
64	  echo "--- HEAD ${PKG_PATH}:${_RBGR_RELIQUARY} ---"
65	  if wget -q -O /dev/null \
66	       --header="Authorization: Bearer ${TOKEN}" \
67	       --header="Accept: ${ACCEPT_MTYPES}" \
68	       "${URL}"; then
69	    echo "  PRESENT: reliquary-tool ${TOOL}"
70	  else
71	    echo "  MISSING: reliquary-tool ${TOOL}"
72	    MISSES="${MISSES} reliquary-tool:${TOOL}"
73	  fi
74	done
75	
76	# Base slots — empty for non-conjure modes; conjure populates from RBRV_IMAGE_n_ANCHOR.
77	# Locator format: <package-path>:<tag>; package path is itself prefixed by the
78	# Lode namespace already (e.g. rbi_ld/<touchmark>:rbi_bole).
79	for SLOT in 1 2 3; do
80	  case "${SLOT}" in
81	    1) LOCATOR="${_RBGR_BASE_LOCATOR_1}" ;;
82	    2) LOCATOR="${_RBGR_BASE_LOCATOR_2}" ;;
83	    3) LOCATOR="${_RBGR_BASE_LOCATOR_3}" ;;
84	  esac
85	  test -n "${LOCATOR}" || continue
86	
87	  case "${LOCATOR}" in
88	    *:*) : ;;
89	    *)   echo "FATAL: invalid base locator format (expected package-path:tag) slot ${SLOT}: ${LOCATOR}" >&2; exit 1 ;;
90	  esac
91	  PKG_PATH="${LOCATOR%:*}"
92	  TAG="${LOCATOR##*:}"
93	  test -n "${PKG_PATH}" || { echo "FATAL: empty package path in locator slot ${SLOT}: ${LOCATOR}" >&2; exit 1; }
94	  test -n "${TAG}"      || { echo "FATAL: empty tag in locator slot ${SLOT}: ${LOCATOR}" >&2; exit 1; }
95	  URL="${REGISTRY_API_BASE}/${PKG_PATH}/manifests/${TAG}"
96	
97	  echo "--- HEAD ${PKG_PATH}:${TAG} (base slot ${SLOT}) ---"
98	  if wget -q -O /dev/null \
99	       --header="Authorization: Bearer ${TOKEN}" \
100	       --header="Accept: ${ACCEPT_MTYPES}" \
101	       "${URL}"; then
102	    echo "  PRESENT: base-anchor slot ${SLOT}"
103	  else
104	    echo "  MISSING: base-anchor slot ${SLOT}"
105	    MISSES="${MISSES} base-anchor:${LOCATOR}"
106	  fi
107	done
108	
109	if [ -z "${MISSES}" ]; then
110	  echo "=== Reliquary preflight passed — pool sees all tools and base anchors ==="
111	  exit 0
112	fi
113	
114	echo "" >&2
115	echo "=== Reliquary preflight FAILED — this pool can't see: ===" >&2
116	for MISS in ${MISSES}; do
117	  echo "  «${MISS}»" >&2
118	done
119	echo "" >&2
120	echo "Defense-in-depth: every Cloud Build verifies reliquary GAR-presence from" >&2
121	echo "the worker pool's vantage. The Director's host-side preflight passed, but" >&2
122	echo "the Mason pool cannot reach one or more required artifacts. Likely causes:" >&2
123	echo "  - airgap pool private-VPC routing missing for these artifacts" >&2
124	echo "  - IAM split between Director and Mason service accounts" >&2
125	echo "  - time skew between host-side preflight and pool-side build execution" >&2
126	echo "" >&2
127	echo "Remediation: re-inscribe reliquary, re-yoke vessel, re-ordain hallmark." >&2
128	exit 1
129

#!/bin/sh
# RBGJR Step 01: In-pool reliquary preflight — HEAD-check tool images and base anchors
# Builder: alpine (from reliquary)
# Substitutions: _RBGR_GAR_HOST, _RBGR_GAR_PATH, _RBGR_RELIQUARIES_ROOT,
#                _RBGR_RELIQUARY,
#                _RBGR_BASE_LOCATOR_1, _RBGR_BASE_LOCATOR_2, _RBGR_BASE_LOCATOR_3
#
# Defense-in-depth: every Cloud Build job validates reliquary GAR-presence
# from the worker pool's vantage before expensive work runs. Catches divergence
# the host-side preflight cannot — airgap pool private-VPC routing, IAM split
# between Director and Mason service accounts, time skew between submission and
# execution.
#
# Coverage:
#   - Reliquary tool images (5 of 6): gcloud, docker, syft, binfmt, skopeo.
#     Alpine (this step's own image) is implicitly validated by Cloud Build
#     pulling it for step execution; failure to pull alpine produces Cloud
#     Build's own "image not pullable" error before this script runs.
#   - Base anchor locators (slots 1/2/3 if non-empty). Conjure populates
#     these; bind and graft pass them through empty.
#
# Failure mode: structured "this pool can't see «TOOL»" message naming each
# missed item. Operator remediation: re-inscribe the reliquary, re-yoke the
# vessel, re-ordain the hallmark.
#
# Auth: Mason SA via Cloud Build metadata server. HEAD requests via busybox
# wget (no curl in alpine by default; apk add not viable on airgap pools).
# wget exit code is the binary signal — 0 for HTTP 2xx, non-zero otherwise.

# POSIX sh (busybox ash on alpine): pipefail is non-portable and not load-bearing
# here — the one pipe (token extract) is validated by the test -n guard below.
set -eu

test -n "${_RBGR_GAR_HOST}"          || { echo "FATAL: _RBGR_GAR_HOST missing"          >&2; exit 1; }
test -n "${_RBGR_GAR_PATH}"          || { echo "FATAL: _RBGR_GAR_PATH missing"          >&2; exit 1; }
test -n "${_RBGR_RELIQUARIES_ROOT}"  || { echo "FATAL: _RBGR_RELIQUARIES_ROOT missing"  >&2; exit 1; }
test -n "${_RBGR_RELIQUARY}"         || { echo "FATAL: _RBGR_RELIQUARY missing"         >&2; exit 1; }

echo "=== In-pool reliquary preflight ==="
echo "Reliquary: ${_RBGR_RELIQUARY}"
echo "GAR base:  ${_RBGR_GAR_HOST}/${_RBGR_GAR_PATH}"

echo "Fetching OAuth2 token from metadata server"
TOKEN_JSON=$(wget -q -O - \
  --header="Metadata-Flavor: Google" \
  "http://metadata.google.internal/computeMetadata/v1/instance/service-accounts/default/token") \
  || { echo "FATAL: Failed to fetch OAuth2 token from metadata server" >&2; exit 1; }

TOKEN=$(printf '%s' "${TOKEN_JSON}" | grep -o '"access_token":"[^"]*"' | cut -d'"' -f4)
test -n "${TOKEN}" || { echo "FATAL: Failed to extract access_token from metadata response" >&2; exit 1; }

ACCEPT_MTYPES="application/vnd.docker.distribution.manifest.v2+json,application/vnd.docker.distribution.manifest.list.v2+json,application/vnd.oci.image.index.v1+json,application/vnd.oci.image.manifest.v1+json"

REGISTRY_API_BASE="https://${_RBGR_GAR_HOST}/v2/${_RBGR_GAR_PATH}"

MISSES=""

# Tool list mirrors the inscribe step manifest (rbgji01) minus alpine.
# Each tool's package path: <RELIQUARIES_ROOT>/<RELIQUARY>/<NAME>; tag = <RELIQUARY>.
for TOOL in gcloud docker syft binfmt skopeo; do
  PKG_PATH="${_RBGR_RELIQUARIES_ROOT}/${_RBGR_RELIQUARY}/${TOOL}"
  URL="${REGISTRY_API_BASE}/${PKG_PATH}/manifests/${_RBGR_RELIQUARY}"

  echo "--- HEAD ${PKG_PATH}:${_RBGR_RELIQUARY} ---"
  if wget -q -O /dev/null \
       --header="Authorization: Bearer ${TOKEN}" \
       --header="Accept: ${ACCEPT_MTYPES}" \
       "${URL}"; then
    echo "  PRESENT: reliquary-tool ${TOOL}"
  else
    echo "  MISSING: reliquary-tool ${TOOL}"
    MISSES="${MISSES} reliquary-tool:${TOOL}"
  fi
done

# Base slots — empty for non-conjure modes; conjure populates from RBRV_IMAGE_n_ANCHOR.
# Locator format: <package-path>:<tag>; package path is itself prefixed by the
# Lode namespace already (e.g. rbi_ld/<touchmark>:rbi_bole).
for SLOT in 1 2 3; do
  case "${SLOT}" in
    1) LOCATOR="${_RBGR_BASE_LOCATOR_1}" ;;
    2) LOCATOR="${_RBGR_BASE_LOCATOR_2}" ;;
    3) LOCATOR="${_RBGR_BASE_LOCATOR_3}" ;;
  esac
  test -n "${LOCATOR}" || continue

  case "${LOCATOR}" in
    *:*) : ;;
    *)   echo "FATAL: invalid base locator format (expected package-path:tag) slot ${SLOT}: ${LOCATOR}" >&2; exit 1 ;;
  esac
  PKG_PATH="${LOCATOR%:*}"
  TAG="${LOCATOR##*:}"
  test -n "${PKG_PATH}" || { echo "FATAL: empty package path in locator slot ${SLOT}: ${LOCATOR}" >&2; exit 1; }
  test -n "${TAG}"      || { echo "FATAL: empty tag in locator slot ${SLOT}: ${LOCATOR}" >&2; exit 1; }
  URL="${REGISTRY_API_BASE}/${PKG_PATH}/manifests/${TAG}"

  echo "--- HEAD ${PKG_PATH}:${TAG} (base slot ${SLOT}) ---"
  if wget -q -O /dev/null \
       --header="Authorization: Bearer ${TOKEN}" \
       --header="Accept: ${ACCEPT_MTYPES}" \
       "${URL}"; then
    echo "  PRESENT: base-anchor slot ${SLOT}"
  else
    echo "  MISSING: base-anchor slot ${SLOT}"
    MISSES="${MISSES} base-anchor:${LOCATOR}"
  fi
done

if [ -z "${MISSES}" ]; then
  echo "=== Reliquary preflight passed — pool sees all tools and base anchors ==="
  exit 0
fi

echo "" >&2
echo "=== Reliquary preflight FAILED — this pool can't see: ===" >&2
for MISS in ${MISSES}; do
  echo "  «${MISS}»" >&2
done
echo "" >&2
echo "Defense-in-depth: every Cloud Build verifies reliquary GAR-presence from" >&2
echo "the worker pool's vantage. The Director's host-side preflight passed, but" >&2
echo "the Mason pool cannot reach one or more required artifacts. Likely causes:" >&2
echo "  - airgap pool private-VPC routing missing for these artifacts" >&2
echo "  - IAM split between Director and Mason service accounts" >&2
echo "  - time skew between host-side preflight and pool-side build execution" >&2
echo "" >&2
echo "Remediation: re-inscribe reliquary, re-yoke vessel, re-ordain hallmark." >&2
exit 1

#!/bin/bash
#
# Copyright 2026 Scale Invariant, Inc.
#
# Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
# you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
# You may obtain a copy of the License at
#
#     http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
#
# Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
# distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
# WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
# See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
# limitations under the License.
#
# Author: Brad Hyslop <bhyslop@scaleinvariant.org>
#
# Recipe Bottle Foundry Ledger - inscribe cluster (guard-free, sourced by rbflk_):
# mirror all tool images from upstream into a datestamped GAR reliquary namespace
# via Cloud Build (Director credentials).

set -euo pipefail

######################################################################
# Inscribe (zrbfl_* / rbfl_*)

# Internal: Submit inscribe Cloud Build job.
# Single step: docker pull each upstream tool image, tag for GAR, push.
# Uses gcr.io/cloud-builders/docker as step image (always pullable — Google-hosted).
zrbfl_inscribe_submit() {
  zrbfl_sentinel

  local -r z_token="${1:?Token required}"
  local -r z_reliquary="${2:?Reliquary datestamp required}"

  buc_step "Constructing inscribe Cloud Build resource"
  local -r z_gar_host="${RBGD_GAR_LOCATION}${RBGC_GAR_HOST_SUFFIX}"
  local -r z_gar_path="${RBGD_GAR_PROJECT_ID}/${RBDC_GAR_REPOSITORY}"
  local -r z_mason_sa="projects/${RBDC_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID}/serviceAccounts/${RBGD_MASON_EMAIL}"

  # Inscribe step image: Google-hosted docker builder (always pullable, even under NO_PUBLIC_EGRESS)
  local -r z_step_image="gcr.io/cloud-builders/docker"

  # Assemble inscribe step from script
  local -r z_script_path="${ZRBFL_RBGJI_STEPS_DIR}/rbgji01-inscribe-mirror.sh"
  test -f "${z_script_path}" || buc_die "Inscribe step script not found: ${z_script_path}"

  local -r z_body_file="${ZRBFL_RELIQUARY_PREFIX}body.txt"
  local -r z_escaped_file="${ZRBFL_RELIQUARY_PREFIX}escaped.txt"

  buc_log_args "Reading inscribe step script (skip shebang)"
  zrbfc_write_script_body "${z_script_path}" "${z_body_file}" \
    || buc_die "Failed to read inscribe step script"
  local z_body=""
  z_body=$(<"${z_body_file}")
  test -n "${z_body}" || buc_die "Empty inscribe script body"

  printf '#!/bin/bash\n%s' "${z_body}" > "${z_escaped_file}" \
    || buc_die "Failed to write escaped inscribe script body"

  local -r z_step_file="${ZRBFL_RELIQUARY_PREFIX}step.json"
  echo "[]" > "${z_step_file}" || buc_die "Failed to initialize inscribe step JSON"

  local -r z_step_built="${ZRBFL_RELIQUARY_PREFIX}step_built.json"
  jq \
    --arg name "${z_step_image}" \
    --arg id "inscribe-mirror" \
    --rawfile script "${z_escaped_file}" \
    '. + [{name: $name, id: $id, script: $script}]' \
    "${z_step_file}" > "${z_step_built}" \
    || buc_die "Failed to build inscribe step JSON"
  mv "${z_step_built}" "${z_step_file}" \
    || buc_die "Failed to finalize inscribe step JSON"

  # Compose Build resource JSON
  buc_log_args "Composing inscribe Build resource JSON"
  local -r z_build_file="${ZRBFL_RELIQUARY_PREFIX}build.json"

  jq -n \
    --slurpfile zjq_steps  "${z_step_file}" \
    --arg zjq_sa           "${z_mason_sa}" \
    --arg zjq_gar_host         "${z_gar_host}" \
    --arg zjq_gar_path         "${z_gar_path}" \
    --arg zjq_reliquaries_root "${RBGL_RELIQUARIES_ROOT}" \
    --arg zjq_reliquary        "${z_reliquary}" \
    --arg zjq_pool             "${RBDC_POOL_TETHER}" \
    --arg zjq_timeout          "${RBRR_GCB_TIMEOUT}" \
    '{
      steps: $zjq_steps[0],
      substitutions: {
        _RBGN_GAR_HOST:         $zjq_gar_host,
        _RBGN_GAR_PATH:         $zjq_gar_path,
        _RBGN_RELIQUARIES_ROOT: $zjq_reliquaries_root,
        _RBGN_RELIQUARY:        $zjq_reliquary
      },
      serviceAccount: $zjq_sa,
      options: {
        automapSubstitutions: true,
        logging: "CLOUD_LOGGING_ONLY",
        pool: { name: $zjq_pool }
      },
      timeout: $zjq_timeout
    }' > "${z_build_file}" \
    || buc_die "Failed to compose inscribe build JSON"

  buc_log_args "Inscribe build JSON: ${z_build_file}"

  rbrd_check "${z_token}"

  buc_step "Submitting inscribe Cloud Build"
  rbuh_json "POST" "${ZRBFC_GCB_PROJECT_BUILDS_URL}" "${z_token}" \
    "reliquary_build_create" "${z_build_file}"
  rbuh_require_ok "Inscribe build submission" "reliquary_build_create"

  local z_build_id=""
  z_build_id=$(rbuh_json_field_capture "reliquary_build_create" '.metadata.build.id') || z_build_id=""
  test -n "${z_build_id}" || buc_die "Build ID not found in builds.create response"
  echo "${z_build_id}" > "${ZRBFC_BUILD_ID_FILE}" || buc_die "Failed to persist build ID"

  local -r z_console_url="${ZRBFC_CLOUD_QUERY_BASE};region=${RBGD_GCB_REGION}/${z_build_id}?project=${RBGD_GCB_PROJECT_ID}"
  buc_info "Inscribe build submitted: ${z_build_id}"
  buc_link "Click to " "Open build in Cloud Console" "${z_console_url}"

  zrbfc_wait_build_completion "${ZRBFC_BUILD_POLL_CEILING_INSCRIBE}" "Inscribe"
}

rbfl_inscribe() {
  zrbfl_sentinel

  buc_doc_brief "Inscribe a reliquary: mirror all tool images from upstream to a datestamped GAR namespace"
  buc_doc_shown || return 0

  # Authenticate as Director
  buc_step "Loading Director RBRA credentials"
  source "${RBDC_DIRECTOR_RBRA_FILE}" || buc_die "Failed to source Director RBRA"

  buc_step "Authenticating as Director"
  local z_token=""
  z_token=$(rbgo_get_token_capture "${RBDC_DIRECTOR_RBRA_FILE}") \
    || buc_die "Failed to get Director OAuth token"

  # Compute reliquary datestamp: r + YYMMDDHHMMSS
  local -r z_reliquary="r${BURD_NOW_STAMP:2:6}${BURD_NOW_STAMP:9:6}"
  buc_info "Reliquary: ${z_reliquary}"

  # Submit inscribe as a Cloud Build job (docker pull/tag/push on GCB)
  zrbfl_inscribe_submit "${z_token}" "${z_reliquary}"

  buf_write_fact_single "${RBF_FACT_RELIQUARY}" "${z_reliquary}"

  buc_success "Inscribe complete — reliquary ${z_reliquary} created"
  buc_info "Add RBRV_RELIQUARY=${z_reliquary} to vessel rbrv.env files to use this reliquary"
}

# eof

content.text: Now the shared snippets and the GAR constants that hold the kind/tag/root values:

input.command: for f in Tools/rbk/rbgjs/rbgjs-token-fetch.sh Tools/rbk/rbgjs/rbgjs-buildx-bootstrap.sh Tools/rbk/rbgjs/rbgjs-buildx-push.sh Tools/rbk/rbgjs/rbgjs-skopeo-fingerprint.sh; do echo "=============== $f ==============="; cat "$f"; done
input.description: Print all four shared snippets

content.content:
=============== Tools/rbk/rbgjs/rbgjs-token-fetch.sh ===============
#!/bin/bash
# RBGJS token-fetch — OAuth2 access token from the Cloud Build metadata server
# (Mason SA). The canonical skopeo-to-GAR authentication (RBSCB): skopeo cannot
# use the gcloud credential helper, so the token is fetched in-memory and never
# written to /workspace, which carries non-secret data only.
#   requires: (none — ambient metadata server)
#   provides: TOKEN
echo "Fetching OAuth2 token from metadata server"
TOKEN_JSON=$(curl -sf -H "Metadata-Flavor: Google" \
  "http://metadata.google.internal/computeMetadata/v1/instance/service-accounts/default/token") \
  || { echo "Failed to fetch OAuth2 token from metadata server" >&2; exit 1; }

TOKEN=$(printf '%s' "${TOKEN_JSON}" | grep -o '"access_token":"[^"]*"' | cut -d'"' -f4)
test -n "${TOKEN}" || { echo "Failed to extract access_token from metadata response" >&2; exit 1; }
=============== Tools/rbk/rbgjs/rbgjs-buildx-bootstrap.sh ===============
#!/bin/bash
# RBGJS buildx-bootstrap — ensure the shared docker-container buildx builder
# exists and is selected. Idempotent under Cloud Build retry: inspect-or-create,
# then use. Run once per step before any push; safe to re-run (a second inspect
# succeeds, create is skipped, use is a no-op).
#   requires: (none)
#   provides: the "rb-builder" buildx builder, created if absent and selected
docker buildx inspect rb-builder >/dev/null 2>&1 \
  || docker buildx create --driver docker-container --name rb-builder
docker buildx use rb-builder
=============== Tools/rbk/rbgjs/rbgjs-buildx-push.sh ===============
#!/bin/bash
# RBGJS buildx-push — push a prepared FROM-scratch build context as an image via
# the shared buildx builder (see buildx-bootstrap). The irreducible push shared
# by the FROM-scratch vouch-push callers (Lode rbgjl02, hallmark-verify rbgjv03):
# push cardinality, the destination URI, the platform set, and the context
# assembly (which JSON, conditional Dockerfile) are all done by the kind.
#   requires: PUSH_URI        full destination image ref including tag
#             PUSH_PLATFORMS  buildx --platform value (e.g. linux/amd64)
#             PUSH_CTX        build context dir holding the Dockerfile
#   provides: the image pushed to PUSH_URI
docker buildx build \
  --push \
  --platform="${PUSH_PLATFORMS}" \
  --tag "${PUSH_URI}" \
  "${PUSH_CTX}" \
  || { echo "FATAL: buildx push failed for ${PUSH_URI}" >&2; exit 1; }
=============== Tools/rbk/rbgjs/rbgjs-skopeo-fingerprint.sh ===============
#!/bin/bash
# RBGJS skopeo-fingerprint — inspect an upstream image's raw manifest, take its
# canonical sha256 digest, and derive the sanitized-origin glance fingerprint.
# The canonical digest is the sha256 of the RAW manifest bytes (what every tool
# reports); the fingerprint is the sanitized origin (`:` and `/` become `-`) plus
# the first 10 hex of that digest — the legacy enshrine anchor form. Only this
# inspect+digest+fingerprint core is identical across the skopeo captures
# (ensconce, enshrine); the first GAR copy is kind-specific (its destination tag
# is itself digest-derived) and stays in the step.
#   requires: ORIGIN    upstream image ref (e.g. docker.io/library/x:tag)
#             RAW_FILE  /workspace path to write the raw manifest into
#   provides: SHA          64-hex canonical digest (no algorithm prefix)
#             FINGERPRINT  "<sanitized-origin>-<first-10-hex>"
skopeo inspect --raw "docker://${ORIGIN}" > "${RAW_FILE}" \
  || { echo "FATAL: Failed to inspect upstream: ${ORIGIN}" >&2; exit 1; }

# sha256sum (coreutils, not openssl) — runs inside the skopeo reliquary container.
SHA=$(sha256sum "${RAW_FILE}" | cut -d' ' -f1)
test -n "${SHA}" || { echo "FATAL: Empty digest for ${ORIGIN}" >&2; exit 1; }

# Sanitize origin (: and / become -), append first 10 hex chars (legacy anchor form).
FINGERPRINT="$(printf '%s' "${ORIGIN}" | tr ':/' '--')-${SHA:0:10}"

=============== Tools/rbk/rbgjs/rbgjs-token-fetch.sh ===============
#!/bin/bash
# RBGJS token-fetch — OAuth2 access token from the Cloud Build metadata server
# (Mason SA). The canonical skopeo-to-GAR authentication (RBSCB): skopeo cannot
# use the gcloud credential helper, so the token is fetched in-memory and never
# written to /workspace, which carries non-secret data only.
#   requires: (none — ambient metadata server)
#   provides: TOKEN
echo "Fetching OAuth2 token from metadata server"
TOKEN_JSON=$(curl -sf -H "Metadata-Flavor: Google" \
  "http://metadata.google.internal/computeMetadata/v1/instance/service-accounts/default/token") \
  || { echo "Failed to fetch OAuth2 token from metadata server" >&2; exit 1; }

TOKEN=$(printf '%s' "${TOKEN_JSON}" | grep -o '"access_token":"[^"]*"' | cut -d'"' -f4)
test -n "${TOKEN}" || { echo "Failed to extract access_token from metadata response" >&2; exit 1; }
=============== Tools/rbk/rbgjs/rbgjs-buildx-bootstrap.sh ===============
#!/bin/bash
# RBGJS buildx-bootstrap — ensure the shared docker-container buildx builder
# exists and is selected. Idempotent under Cloud Build retry: inspect-or-create,
# then use. Run once per step before any push; safe to re-run (a second inspect
# succeeds, create is skipped, use is a no-op).
#   requires: (none)
#   provides: the "rb-builder" buildx builder, created if absent and selected
docker buildx inspect rb-builder >/dev/null 2>&1 \
  || docker buildx create --driver docker-container --name rb-builder
docker buildx use rb-builder
=============== Tools/rbk/rbgjs/rbgjs-buildx-push.sh ===============
#!/bin/bash
# RBGJS buildx-push — push a prepared FROM-scratch build context as an image via
# the shared buildx builder (see buildx-bootstrap). The irreducible push shared
# by the FROM-scratch vouch-push callers (Lode rbgjl02, hallmark-verify rbgjv03):
# push cardinality, the destination URI, the platform set, and the context
# assembly (which JSON, conditional Dockerfile) are all done by the kind.
#   requires: PUSH_URI        full destination image ref including tag
#             PUSH_PLATFORMS  buildx --platform value (e.g. linux/amd64)
#             PUSH_CTX        build context dir holding the Dockerfile
#   provides: the image pushed to PUSH_URI
docker buildx build \
  --push \
  --platform="${PUSH_PLATFORMS}" \
  --tag "${PUSH_URI}" \
  "${PUSH_CTX}" \
  || { echo "FATAL: buildx push failed for ${PUSH_URI}" >&2; exit 1; }
=============== Tools/rbk/rbgjs/rbgjs-skopeo-fingerprint.sh ===============
#!/bin/bash
# RBGJS skopeo-fingerprint — inspect an upstream image's raw manifest, take its
# canonical sha256 digest, and derive the sanitized-origin glance fingerprint.
# The canonical digest is the sha256 of the RAW manifest bytes (what every tool
# reports); the fingerprint is the sanitized origin (`:` and `/` become `-`) plus
# the first 10 hex of that digest — the legacy enshrine anchor form. Only this
# inspect+digest+fingerprint core is identical across the skopeo captures
# (ensconce, enshrine); the first GAR copy is kind-specific (its destination tag
# is itself digest-derived) and stays in the step.
#   requires: ORIGIN    upstream image ref (e.g. docker.io/library/x:tag)
#             RAW_FILE  /workspace path to write the raw manifest into
#   provides: SHA          64-hex canonical digest (no algorithm prefix)
#             FINGERPRINT  "<sanitized-origin>-<first-10-hex>"
skopeo inspect --raw "docker://${ORIGIN}" > "${RAW_FILE}" \
  || { echo "FATAL: Failed to inspect upstream: ${ORIGIN}" >&2; exit 1; }

# sha256sum (coreutils, not openssl) — runs inside the skopeo reliquary container.
SHA=$(sha256sum "${RAW_FILE}" | cut -d' ' -f1)
test -n "${SHA}" || { echo "FATAL: Empty digest for ${ORIGIN}" >&2; exit 1; }

# Sanitize origin (: and / become -), append first 10 hex chars (legacy anchor form).
FINGERPRINT="$(printf '%s' "${ORIGIN}" | tr ':/' '--')-${SHA:0:10}"

14	# See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
15	# limitations under the License.
16	#
17	# Author: Brad Hyslop <bhyslop@scaleinvariant.org>
18	#
19	# Recipe Bottle GCP Constants - Implementation (no printf required)
20	
21	set -euo pipefail
22	
23	# Multiple inclusion detection
24	# (Module state remains ZRBGC_* per BCG; external constants use RBGC_*)
25	test -z "${ZRBGC_SOURCED:-}" || buc_die "Module rbgc multiply sourced - check sourcing hierarchy"
26	ZRBGC_SOURCED=1
27	
28	# Tinder constants (pure string literals, no variable expansion — available at source time)
29	# Depot project ID infix between RBRD_CLOUD_PREFIX and RBRD_DEPOT_MONIKER, consumed
30	# by rbdc_DerivedConstants.sh's RBDC_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID derivation.
31	RBGC_depot_project_infix="d-"
32	
33	######################################################################
34	# Internal Functions (zrbgc_*)
35	
36	zrbgc_kindle() {
37	  test -z "${ZRBGC_KINDLED:-}" || buc_die "Module rbgc already kindled"
38	
39	  # Global Resource Naming (Google Cloud global namespace)
40	  # These resources compete in globally-unique namespaces across all of GCP
41	  # Pattern: {prefix}-{type}-{name}-{timestamp} where timestamp is YYMMDDHHMMSS
42	  readonly RBGC_GLOBAL_PREFIX="rbwg"
43	  readonly RBGC_GLOBAL_TYPE_PAYOR="p"
44	  readonly RBGC_GLOBAL_TYPE_DEPOT="d"
45	  readonly RBGC_GLOBAL_TYPE_BUCKET="b"
46	  readonly RBGC_GLOBAL_TIMESTAMP_FORMAT="+%y%m%d%H%M%S"
47	  readonly RBGC_GLOBAL_TIMESTAMP_LEN=12
48	  readonly RBGC_GLOBAL_TIMESTAMP_REGEX="[0-9]{${RBGC_GLOBAL_TIMESTAMP_LEN}}"
49	
50	  # Global resource validation patterns
51	  # Payor:  rbwg-p-YYMMDDHHMMSS  (timestamp survives — payor is installation-scoped, not depot-scoped)
52	  readonly RBGC_GLOBAL_PAYOR_REGEX="^${RBGC_GLOBAL_PREFIX}-${RBGC_GLOBAL_TYPE_PAYOR}-${RBGC_GLOBAL_TIMESTAMP_REGEX}$"
53	
54	  # Basic Configuration
55	  readonly RBGC_ADMIN_ROLE="rbw-admin"
56	  readonly RBGC_PAYOR_ROLE="rbw-payor"
57	  readonly RBGC_PAYOR_APP_NAME="Recipe Bottle Payor"
58	
59	  # Timeouts
60	  readonly RBGC_MAX_CONSISTENCY_SEC=90
61	  readonly RBGC_EVENTUAL_CONSISTENCY_SEC=3
62	  # Consecutive 404s rbuh_poll_until_gone requires before declaring a deleted
63	  # resource durably gone. GCP IAM's SA read path is multi-replica eventually-
64	  # consistent: a post-DELETE GET flaps 200<->404 for seconds as replicas
65	  # converge, so a single 404 is not durable proof — a same-name recreate can
66	  # still race a lagging replica's existence preflight. The streak debounces
67	  # that flap; any intervening 200 resets it. Bounded by RBGC_MAX_CONSISTENCY_SEC.
68	  readonly RBGC_GONE_CONFIRM_STREAK=3
69	  readonly RBGC_SA_KEY_CREATE_RETRY_MAX=7
70	  readonly RBGC_SA_KEY_CREATE_RETRY_DELAY_SEC=10
71	
72	  # JWT-bearer consumer-side retry — every RBRA consumer absorbs the post-write
73	  # race where Google's OAuth backend has not yet accepted a freshly-minted SA
74	  # or its key, surfaced as `invalid_grant` paired with either
75	  # `Invalid JWT Signature.` (fresh-key lag) or `Invalid grant: account not
76	  # found` (fresh-SA lag). 90s budget locked by ₣BB pristine-tier contract;
77	  # cadence below.
78	  readonly RBGC_SA_KEY_CONSUMER_RETRY_BUDGET_SEC=90
79	  readonly RBGC_SA_KEY_CONSUMER_RETRY_INITIAL_DELAY_SEC=2
80	  readonly RBGC_SA_KEY_CONSUMER_RETRY_MAX_DELAY_SEC=15
81	
82	  # IAM-grant propagation retry — exponential-backoff budget shared by every
83	  # get-modify-set IAM grant site in rbgi_IAM.sh plus the inline GAR retry in
84	  # rbgg_invest_director. Recognizes three propagation classes against the
85	  # same time budget: (A) forward member-visibility (HTTP 400 "does not
86	  # exist"), (B) backward member-visibility (HTTP 400 "is not deleted"),
87	  # (C) caller-recently-empowered (HTTP 403 from resource-scope IAM caches).
88	  # Class C is time-bounded only — real propagation succeeds within budget,
89	  # real denial waits the budget and fails cleanly. RBSCIP locks the profile.
90	  readonly RBGC_PROPAGATION_INITIAL_DELAY_SEC=3
91	  readonly RBGC_PROPAGATION_MAX_DELAY_SEC=20
92	  readonly RBGC_PROPAGATION_DEADLINE_SEC=420
93	
94	  # HTTP transient-failure retry — bounded retry on curl-network blips
95	  # (connection refused 7, timeout 28, TLS handshake 35, recv failure 56).
96	  # Shared by rbuh_json and the OAuth token-mint POST. Other curl exits
97	  # are configuration-deterministic and fail fast.
98	  readonly RBGC_HTTP_TRANSIENT_RETRY_ATTEMPTS=3
99	  readonly RBGC_HTTP_TRANSIENT_RETRY_SLEEP_SEC=3
100	
101	  # docker login daemon->registry transient — the moby/moby#44350 signature.
102	  # docker login's registry-auth client carries a hardcoded, non-configurable
103	  # 15s timeout (moby/registry/auth.go); against a healthy-but-slow GAR auth
104	  # backend it fires prematurely, emitting this Go net/http stdlib string.
105	  # Alone among login/pull/push, login carries no internal retry, so callers
106	  # wrap it (rbgo_docker_login, zrbndb_docker_login) reusing the HTTP retry
107	  # budget above. The string is a Go standard-library invariant, stable across
108	  # docker versions; this is a surveyed-signature allowlist, NOT a catch-all —
109	  # real auth failures emit "unauthorized" and fail fast.
110	  readonly RBGC_DOCKER_LOGIN_TRANSIENT_SIGNATURE='Client.Timeout exceeded while awaiting headers'
111	
112	  # docker login credential-persist failure under headless Cygwin. Docker
113	  # Desktop's docker-credential-wincred backend cannot reach the Windows
114	  # Credential Manager from an sshd session that owns no interactive logon
115	  # (cmdkey /list is empty over SSH), emitting this Win32 string (rc=1) AFTER
116	  # auth has already succeeded — the token mint and the push path are sound;
117	  # only the credential STORE fails. Windows docker also ignores an empty
118	  # credsStore (the CLI still detects wincred), so config alone cannot divert
119	  # the store to the file store. At this Pale (docker's own credential store,
120	  # source we cannot edit) rbgo_docker_login bends ONCE on this exact signature:
121	  # since auth already succeeded it writes the credential into the base64 file
122	  # store itself (the config.json `auths` map docker push reads directly — the
123	  # form WSL uses natively) and treats login as done. A real auth failure emits
124	  # "unauthorized" and never matches. Stable Win32 system-error message;
125	  # corroborated by docker/cli#4353 and #1263. REMOVE the bend when the
126	  # uncontrolled-Cygwin host gains an interactive Windows logon / working vault.
127	  readonly RBGC_DOCKER_WINCRED_HEADLESS_SIGNATURE='A specified logon session does not exist'
128	
129	  # URL Roots & Well-known Endpoints
130	  readonly RBGC_OAUTH_TOKEN_URL="https://oauth2.googleapis.com/token"
131	  readonly RBGC_OAUTH_AUTHORIZE_URL="https://accounts.google.com/o/oauth2/v2/auth"
132	  readonly RBGC_OAUTH_USERINFO_URL="https://www.googleapis.com/oauth2/v3/userinfo"
133	  readonly RBGC_API_ROOT_IAM="https://iam.googleapis.com"
134	  readonly RBGC_API_ROOT_CRM="https://cloudresourcemanager.googleapis.com"
135	  readonly RBGC_API_ROOT_SERVICEUSAGE="https://serviceusage.googleapis.com"
136	  readonly RBGC_API_ROOT_ARTIFACTREGISTRY="https://artifactregistry.googleapis.com"
137	  readonly RBGC_API_ROOT_CLOUDBUILD="https://cloudbuild.googleapis.com"
138	  readonly RBGC_API_ROOT_CLOUDBILLING="https://cloudbilling.googleapis.com"
139	  readonly RBGC_API_ROOT_STORAGE="https://storage.googleapis.com"
140	  readonly RBGC_API_ROOT_SECRETMANAGER="https://secretmanager.googleapis.com"
141	  readonly RBGC_CONSOLE_URL="https://console.cloud.google.com/"
142	  readonly RBGC_SIGNUP_URL="https://cloud.google.com/free"
143	
144	  # OAuth Scopes
145	  readonly RBGC_SCOPE_CLOUD_PLATFORM="https://www.googleapis.com/auth/cloud-platform"
146	
147	  # Service Usage Service Identifiers
148	  readonly RBGC_SERVICE_IAM="iam.googleapis.com"
149	  readonly RBGC_SERVICE_CRM="cloudresourcemanager.googleapis.com"
150	  readonly RBGC_SERVICE_ARTIFACTREGISTRY="artifactregistry.googleapis.com"
151	
152	  # Email/Domain Assembly
153	  readonly RBGC_SA_EMAIL_DOMAIN="iam.gserviceaccount.com"
154	
155	  # API Version Paths
156	  readonly RBGC_IAM_V1="/v1"
157	  readonly RBGC_CRM_V1="/v1"
158	  readonly RBGC_CRM_V3="/v3"
159	  readonly RBGC_SERVICEUSAGE_V1="/v1"
160	  readonly RBGC_SERVICEUSAGE_V1BETA1="/v1beta1"
161	  readonly RBGC_ARTIFACTREGISTRY_V1="/v1"
162	  readonly RBGC_CLOUDBUILD_V1="/v1"
163	  readonly RBGC_CLOUDBILLING_V1="/v1"
164	  readonly RBGC_STORAGE_JSON_V1="/storage/v1"
165	  readonly RBGC_STORAGE_JSON_UPLOAD="/upload/storage/v1"
166	  readonly RBGC_SECRETMANAGER_V1="/v1"
167	
168	  # REST Path Fragments
169	  readonly RBGC_PATH_PROJECTS="/projects"
170	  readonly RBGC_PATH_LOCATIONS="/locations"
171	  readonly RBGC_PATH_REPOSITORIES="/repositories"
172	  readonly RBGC_PATH_SERVICE_ACCOUNTS="/serviceAccounts"
173	  readonly RBGC_PATH_KEYS="/keys"
174	
175	  # REST Operation Suffixes
176	  readonly RBGC_CRM_GET_IAM_POLICY_SUFFIX=":getIamPolicy"
177	  readonly RBGC_CRM_SET_IAM_POLICY_SUFFIX=":setIamPolicy"
178	  readonly RBGC_SERVICEUSAGE_ENABLE_SUFFIX=":enable"
179	  readonly RBGC_SERVICEUSAGE_PATH_SERVICES="/services"
180	
181	  # Operation Prefixes
182	  readonly RBGC_OP_PREFIX_GLOBAL="operations/"
183	
184	  # Ark Artifact Basenames (₢A_AAK layout)
185	  # Each ark type is a plain basename sibling under rbi_hm/<hallmark>/.
186	  readonly RBGC_ARK_BASENAME_IMAGE="image"
187	  readonly RBGC_ARK_BASENAME_ABOUT="about"
188	  readonly RBGC_ARK_BASENAME_VOUCH="vouch"
189	  readonly RBGC_ARK_BASENAME_DIAGS="diags"
190	  readonly RBGC_ARK_BASENAME_ATTEST="attest"
191	  readonly RBGC_ARK_BASENAME_POUCH="pouch"
192	
193	  # Hallmark Prefix Letters
194	  # Encode artifact provenance in the leading character of a hallmark stamp.
195	  # Kludge hallmarks are local-only; the other three originate in GAR.
196	  readonly RBGC_HALLMARK_PREFIX_CONJURE="c"
197	  readonly RBGC_HALLMARK_PREFIX_KLUDGE="k"
198	  readonly RBGC_HALLMARK_PREFIX_BIND="b"
199	  readonly RBGC_HALLMARK_PREFIX_GRAFT="g"
200	
201	  # GAR Categorical Namespaces (₢A_AAK layout)
202	  # Top-level namespaces under which arks are stored. Consumed by rbgl_GarLayout.sh.
203	  # The hm/rq siblings hold Director-authored image families; rbi_df holds
204	  # Payor-authored depot-scoped OCI artifacts produced during depot lifetime.
205	  readonly RBGC_GAR_CATEGORY_HALLMARKS="rbi_hm"
206	  readonly RBGC_GAR_CATEGORY_RELIQUARIES="rbi_rq"
207	  readonly RBGC_GAR_CATEGORY_DEPOT_FACTS="rbi_df"
208	  readonly RBGC_GAR_CATEGORY_LODES="rbi_ld"
209	
210	  # GAR Lode Layout (fetched-side universal capture — see RBSL)
211	  # One Lode = one GAR package named rbi_ld/<kind-letter><stamp>; that package
212	  # IS the atomic-delete unit (single `packages delete`). Members and provenance
213	  # ride as TAGS within the one package, never as /-path-segments (GAR has no
214	  # subtree delete). Stamp matches the hallmark second-granular form YYMMDDHHMMSS.
215	  #
216	  # Kind letters (one per capture kind; podvm carries two for its quay families):
217	  #   b  bole | t  tool | r  reliquary | w  wsl | vw  podvm-wsl | vn  podvm-native
218	  readonly RBGC_LODE_KIND_BOLE="b"
219	  readonly RBGC_LODE_KIND_TOOL="t"
220	  readonly RBGC_LODE_KIND_RELIQUARY="r"
221	  readonly RBGC_LODE_KIND_WSL="w"
222	  readonly RBGC_LODE_KIND_PODVM_WSL="vw"
223	  readonly RBGC_LODE_KIND_PODVM_NATIVE="vn"
224	
225	  # Kind-brand enum — the touchmark's kind carried in the host-side single-form
226	  # chaining fact a derived-pull election reads to resolve the member tag. It is
227	  # read as its own fact, NOT parsed from the touchmark's kind-letter prefix
228	  # (the chaining channel is single-form: opaque values, never parsed). Only the
229	  # landed bole kind today; later kinds add their brand here with their vertical.
230	  readonly RBGC_LODE_BRAND_BOLE="bole"
231	
232	  # Member/provenance tags. The rbi_ sprue marks strings from RB's domain:
233	  # RB's authored lexicon (bole, vouch) and RB-measured-from-content values
234	  # (the digest). It does NOT mark foreign-cued strings — the sanitized-origin
235	  # tag is UNSPRUED (origin is a vessel cue), computed at capture, not a constant.
236	  readonly RBGC_LODE_TAG_BOLE="rbi_bole"            # uniform greppable handle (bole singleton)
237	  readonly RBGC_LODE_TAG_VOUCH="rbi_vouch"          # one-per-Lode provenance envelope
238	  readonly RBGC_LODE_TAG_DIGEST_PREFIX="rbi_sha256-"  # canonical OCI digest tag: rbi_sha256-<full-hex>
239	
240	  # Provenance envelope (:rbi_vouch) — two honest trust grades, declared per Lode.
241	  # bole captures the durable-upstream grade; podvm-* will carry the recorded grade.
242	  readonly RBGC_LODE_TRUST_VERIFIED="verified-against-published"
243	  readonly RBGC_LODE_TRUST_RECORDED="recorded-at-acquisition"
244	  readonly RBGC_LODE_VOUCH_SCHEMA="rbld-vouch-1"    # near-term unsigned, schema-versioned
245	
246	  # rbi_df layout: flat namespace. No subdirs. Each filename names one
247	  # depot-scoped artifact; tag varies by artifact role.
248	  #
249	  # Current artifacts:
250	  #   probe-tether:probe   — tether pool levy-time capability probe
251	  #                          (cloud-pushed marker image, FROM scratch)
252	  #   probe-airgap:probe   — airgap pool levy-time capability probe
253	  #                          (cloud-pushed marker image, FROM scratch)
254	  #   rbrd:tripwire        — depot regime tripwire (FROM-scratch image
255	  #                          carrying rbmm_moorings/rbrd.env). Host-inscribed by
256	  #                          Payor at end of levy; pulled + byte-diffed
257	  #                          by every cloud-submitting command. See
258	  #                          Tools/rbk/rbndb_base.sh.
259	  #
260	  # The depot-time-immutable identity and pool settings (CLOUD_PREFIX,
261	  # DEPOT_MONIKER, GCP_REGION, GCB_MACHINE_TYPE) live in the RBRD regime
262	  # and are also inscribed into rbi_df at the rbrd:tripwire tag so post-
263	  # levy drift can be detected at every subsequent cloud submission.
264	  #
265	  # Enumerators (rbw-iah / rbw-iar) ignore rbi_df by design — its
266	  # contents are operational, not part of the hallmark/reliquary
267	  # image catalogue.
268	
269	  # Reliquary Tool Basenames (₢A_AAK layout)
270	  # Canonical tool names under rbi_rq/<date>/. Authoritative
271	  # manifest lives in rbgji/rbgji01-inscribe-mirror.sh.
272	  readonly RBGC_RELIQUARY_TOOL_GCLOUD="gcloud"
273	  readonly RBGC_RELIQUARY_TOOL_DOCKER="docker"
274	  readonly RBGC_RELIQUARY_TOOL_ALPINE="alpine"
275	  readonly RBGC_RELIQUARY_TOOL_SYFT="syft"
276	  readonly RBGC_RELIQUARY_TOOL_BINFMT="binfmt"
277	  readonly RBGC_RELIQUARY_TOOL_SKOPEO="skopeo"
278	
279	  # Fact-file filenames (written to BURD_OUTPUT_DIR by producers, read by tests)
280	  readonly RBF_FACT_HALLMARK="rbf_fact_hallmark"
281	  readonly RBF_FACT_BUILD_ID="rbf_fact_build_id"
282	  readonly RBF_FACT_GAR_ROOT="rbf_fact_gar_root"
283	  readonly RBF_FACT_ARK_STEM="rbf_fact_ark_stem"
284	  readonly RBF_FACT_ARK_YIELD="rbf_fact_ark_yield"
285	  readonly RBF_FACT_RELIQUARY="rbf_fact_reliquary"
286	
287	  # Lode capture chaining facts (single-form, fixed filenames). Ensconce is
288	  # capture-pure and writes no consumer config; it hands the bole touchmark to a
289	  # later derived-pull election (the conjure ANCHOR populator) through these two
290	  # bare facts via the depth-1 cross-tabtarget chain. The provenance envelope
291	  # lives only in GAR (:rbi_vouch), never host-side. TOUCHMARK carries the Lode
292	  # stamp (e.g. b260602120000); BRAND carries the kind enum (RBGC_LODE_BRAND_*).
293	  readonly RBF_FACT_LODE_TOUCHMARK="rbf_fact_lode_touchmark"
294	  readonly RBF_FACT_LODE_BRAND="rbf_fact_lode_brand"
295	
296	  # Payor fact-file filenames (governor identifying values)
297	  readonly RBGP_FACT_GOVERNOR_SA_EMAIL="rbgp_fact_governor_sa_email"
298	
299	  # Depot lifecycle-state vocabulary. Fact-file extensions live in RBCC.
300	  # rbgp_depot_list emits one fact file per known depot at
301	  # "<cloud_prefix>/<moniker>.${RBCC_fact_ext_depot}" with content equal to
302	  # one of the values below. The cloud_prefix subdir prevents collisions
303	  # between same-moniker depots under different cloud_prefixes.
304	  readonly RBGP_DEPOT_STATE_COMPLETE="COMPLETE"
305	  readonly RBGP_DEPOT_STATE_DELETE_REQUESTED="DELETE_REQUESTED"
306	
307	  # DisplayName anchor used across depot-creation sites (depot project,
308	  # Mason SA, Governor SA). Search backend filters CRM v3 projects:search
309	  # by displayName starting with this anchor. Distinct, unmistakable string
310	  # ensures no collision with non-depot projects in the operator's account.
311	  readonly RBGC_DEPOT_DISPLAY_PREFIX="RBGC-DEPOT"
312	
313	  # Artifact Registry (GAR) Composition
314	  readonly RBGC_GAR_HOST_SUFFIX="-docker.pkg.dev"
315	
316	  # GAR Cleanup Policy (applied at depot levy — see RBSDE "Create Container Repository").
317	  # Reaps untagged manifests on GAR's daily cleanup cadence; underwrites the V2-DELETE-by-tag
318	  # contract documented in RBSIJ for multi-platform orphan children.
319	  readonly RBGC_GAR_CLEANUP_POLICY_ID="rb-delete-untagged"
320	  readonly RBGC_GAR_CLEANUP_OLDER_THAN_SEC="86400s"
321	
322	
323	  # Canonical Role IDs
324	  readonly RBGC_ROLE_ARTIFACTREGISTRY_READER="roles/artifactregistry.reader"
325	  readonly RBGC_ROLE_ARTIFACTREGISTRY_WRITER="roles/artifactregistry.writer"
326	  readonly RBGC_ROLE_ARTIFACTREGISTRY_ADMIN="roles/artifactregistry.admin"
327	  readonly RBGC_ROLE_CONTAINERANALYSIS_OCCURRENCES_VIEWER="roles/containeranalysis.occurrences.viewer"
328	  readonly RBGC_ROLE_CLOUDBUILD_BUILDS_EDITOR="roles/cloudbuild.builds.editor"
329	
330	  # Common API Base Paths (project-independent)
331	  readonly RBGC_API_BASE_GCS="${RBGC_API_ROOT_STORAGE}${RBGC_STORAGE_JSON_V1}"
332	
333	  # Cloud Resource Manager - Liens API
334	  readonly RBGC_API_CRM_LIST_LIENS="${RBGC_API_ROOT_CRM}${RBGC_CRM_V1}/liens"
335	  readonly RBGC_API_CRM_DELETE_LIEN="${RBGC_API_ROOT_CRM}${RBGC_CRM_V1}/liens"
336	
337	  # Google Cloud Storage (GCS) APIs (project-independent)
338	  readonly RBGC_API_GCS_BUCKETS="${RBGC_API_BASE_GCS}/b"
339	
340	  readonly RBGC_BUILD_RUNNER_PLATFORM="linux/amd64"
341	
342	
343	  # Worker pool infrastructure (dual pools: tether + airgap)
344	  readonly RBGC_POOL_SUFFIX_TETHER="-tether"
345	  readonly RBGC_POOL_SUFFIX_AIRGAP="-airgap"
346	  readonly RBGC_WORKER_POOL_SUFFIX="-pool"
347	  readonly RBGC_PATH_WORKER_POOLS="/workerPools"
348	
349	  readonly ZRBGC_KINDLED=1
350	}
351	
352	zrbgc_sentinel() {
353	  test "${ZRBGC_KINDLED:-}" = "1" || buc_die "Module rbgc not kindled - call zrbgc_kindle first"
354	}
355	
356	# eof
357	
358
